stem:
-7, 4, -2, 13

A data set of four numbers is shown. If a number from this data set is selected at random, what is the probability of selecting a positive number?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. There are 2 positive numbers in the data set (4 and 13) out of 4 total numbers, so the probability is 2/4 = 1/2.

Choice B is incorrect; this would be the probability of selecting one specific number. Choice C is incorrect; this is the probability of selecting a negative number. Choice D is incorrect; this would mean all numbers are positive.
